title: viola × tuvinica
intro: Viola × tuvinica, also known as the viola hybrid, is a unique plant resulting from the cross-breeding of Viola adunca and Viola biflora. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Viola × tuvinica include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, protect against cell damage, and promote overall well-being. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
